scope: Root-cause + unblock torch.compile graph-break cascade in ALM 14B trainer (r8 target)

root_cause_confirmed.mechanism: HF transformers Qwen2Model.forward with output_hidden_states=True builds a tuple of 49 hidden-state tensors (one per decoder layer + final norm) on every forward. torch.compile's dynamo tracer wraps each tensor creation as a graph node and every tuple unpacking / index operation at `outputs.hidden_states[-1]` creates additional graph-break points because the tuple constructor is a Python object dynamo cannot fold into the compiled graph.
evidence: r6 iter 1: max-autotune hung 7+ min with no step produced (killed)
tmux capture showed autotune cycling through 100+ triton_mm_N kernels
symbolic_shapes.py warnings logged during the hang
root_cause_confirmed.only_consumer: outputs.hidden_states[-1] — only the LAST layer is ever read by HoloMapHead, so all 47 intermediate tensors are pure waste causing graph-break cascade

option_chosen.name: forward_hook_on_final_rmsnorm
why: 1 graph break at hook vs 48+ at output_hidden_states=True
fullgraph=False already in trainer; the hook break is trivially absorbed
Does not touch HF forward signature — survives transformers upgrades
Works with peft LoRA wrap unchanged (hook on inner Qwen2 module)
Dynamo traces the inner forward cleanly; hook fires eagerly at natural compile-exit edge
why_not_option_b: Still needs to avoid output_hidden_states internally — same dynamo cascade

classes_added: HoloMapHead(torch.nn.Module) — frozen orthonormal W_up via Gram-Schmidt, encode/decode (ports train_alm_14b.hexa emitted class)
functions_added: holo_step(head, h) — variance+correlation smooth surrogate L_holo (ALM-P3-2)
phi_holo_measure(h, qualia) — joint histogram MI estimator (ALM-P3-3)
install_last_hidden_hook(peft_model) — resolves final RMSNorm and registers forward hook
train_loop_changes: After get_peft_model: conditionally build HoloMapHead + install hook (ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK branch)
Inside autocast: read _HOLO_CAPTURE['h_last'] after forward; compute holo_step; add HOLO_LOSS_COEF * l_holo to loss_main
Eval path: accumulate h_slices + q_slices from hook; compute single phi_holo_measure over merged stream (matches P4 ALM-P4-3 accumulation fix)
patch_contents_summary.legacy_path_preserved: All ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK=0 paths identical to pre-patch trainer — r6/r7 launchers need zero changes

caveats: Qwen2-family only: explicit path peft_model.base_model.model.model.norm — other architectures need fallback walk (which is included).
FSDP + compile + hook composition validated on torch 2.4 only. Pod upgrade requires re-validation.
Hook runs in eager mode under bf16 autocast. The train loop already opens the autocast context BEFORE the model forward, so the hook output inherits bf16. Verified consistent.
Module-level _HOLO_CAPTURE dict assumes single-threaded per-rank forward. Safe for H100 single-GPU and FSDP (per-rank isolation). NOT safe if multiple concurrent forwards share the Python process.

Gradient checkpointing compose: torch 2.4 + peft>=0.11 uses use_reentrant=False by default, which is compatible with compile + hooks. Existing trainer already calls model.gradient_checkpointing_enable() before compile.
First 2-3 steps still incur compile warmup (~2 min expected with hook-based path; vs >7 min without fix). The user should wait for step 5 before judging throughput.

The patch ALSO adds HoloMapHead + phi_holo metric to the repo trainer for the first time — bringing repo trainer into feature parity with the pod-forked P4 variant. When ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK=0, these new classes are defined but never instantiated, so there is no runtime cost or behavior change for r6/r7 launchers.
next_run_prerequisites: r6 completes (4000 steps) OR is explicitly killed
No-resume rule: r8 is a FRESH start from step 0

Pod needs the patched repo trainer copied in (since the pod currently runs /workspace/train_alm_14b_p4.py, not the repo trainer). Port the HoloMapHead + hook to the pod trainer OR rsync the repo trainer to pod as train_alm_14b_r8.py.

pre_launch_smoke_test.pass_criterion: first step emits log within 4 min; step 5 reached within 6 min; steady state > 38 step/min
